perturbation.original_text: 1. SUBJECT OF THE CONTRACT 1.1 The Company, at the expense and on the instructions of the Principal, shall arrange transportation for export in batches to the far abroad of crude oil ("Commodity") belonging to the Principal and shall provide such other services in connection therewith as are provided in this Contract. 1.2 The volume of a batch of Commodity transported hereunder shall be determined in accordance with the monthly schedules of transit and distribution of Kazakhstany oil.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. SUBJECT OF THE CONTRACT 1.1 The Company, at the expense and on the instructions of the Principal, shall arrange transportation for export in batches to the far abroad of crude oil ("Product") belonging to the Principal and shall provide such other services in connection therewith as are provided in this Contract. 1.2 The volume of a batch of Commodity transported hereunder shall be determined in accordance with the monthly schedules of transit and distribution of Kazakhstany oil.
perturbation.explanation: In section 1, the key term 'Commodity' is changed to 'Product' in subsection 1.1, while it remains 'Commodity' in subsection 1.2. This creates an in-text contradiction, as it is unclear whether 'Commodity' and 'Product' refer to the same material. This affects the entire contract by creating uncertainty about the subject of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.2 The volume of a batch of Commodity transported hereunder shall be determined in accordance with the monthly schedules of transit and distribution of Kazakhstany oil.
perturbation.justification: The change in section 1.1 from 'Commodity' to 'Product' creates a direct contradiction with section 1.2, which still uses 'Commodity'. This misalignment of terminology creates uncertainty about whether both terms refer to the same subject matter of the contract, leading to potential disputes regarding which term governs the obligations and rights of the parties. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', specifically the use of multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 Unless otherwise agreed by the Parties, the Principal shall at all times deliver to the Company Karakuduk Commodity pursuant to this Contract. The Company shall ensure that (i) during the Principal Period, the quality of Commodity delivered at the relevant delivery point shall be REBCO, and (ii) during the Secondary Period, the quality of Commodity delivered at the CPC Terminal shall be Karakuduk Commodity for segregated batch deliveries or shall be CPC Blend for deliveries for which segregated batch delivery is not available.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 Unless otherwise agreed by the Parties, the Principal shall at all times deliver to the Company Karakuduk Feedstock pursuant to this Contract. The Company shall ensure that (i) during the Principal Period, the quality of Commodity delivered at the relevant delivery point shall be REBCO, and (ii) during the Secondary Period, the quality of Commodity delivered at the CPC Terminal shall be Karakuduk Commodity for segregated batch deliveries or shall be CPC Blend for deliveries for which segregated batch delivery is not available.
perturbation.explanation: In section 3.1, 'Commodity' is changed to 'Feedstock' in the first sentence. However, the rest of the paragraph still refers to 'Commodity'. This creates an in-text contradiction because it's unclear if 'Feedstock' and 'Commodity' are the same thing. This change will make it confusing to enforce quality standards
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 Unless otherwise agreed by the Parties, the Principal shall at all times deliver to the Company Karakuduk Feedstock pursuant to this Contract. The Company shall ensure that (i) during the Principal Period, the quality of Commodity delivered at the relevant delivery point shall be REBCO, and (ii) during the Secondary Period, the quality of Commodity delivered at the CPC Terminal shall be Karakuduk Commodity for segregated batch deliveries or shall be CPC Blend for deliveries for which segregated batch delivery is not available.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Commodity' with 'Feedstock' in the first sentence of section 3.1, while the rest of the paragraph still refers to 'Commodity'. This creates an in-text contradiction. The contradiction exists because the meaning of Commodity vs Feedstock is undefined and not used interchangeably. The change therefore creates ambiguity regarding the quality standards. This misalignment in terminology leads to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 At all times during the term of this Contract, the Company shall: (i) Assist in obtaining required&sbsp;official export permissions (certificate of origin of the Commodity at place the load output, customs declaration on Commodity output) for release of the batch of Commodity being delivered from the customs territory of the Republic of Kazakhstan; 3 (ii) accept Karakuduk Commodity from the Principal at Metering Point 719 (PSP Samara of the Western branch office of KazTransOil) and arrange its transportation for export in accordance with the Delivery Basis; (iii) execute all customs formalities to carry out transit transportation of the batch of Commodity through the territory of Russian Federation and the countries of the C.I.S.;
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 At all times during the term of this Contract, the Company shall: (i) Assist in obtaining required&sbsp;official export permissions (certificate of origin of the Oil at place the load output, customs declaration on Commodity output) for release of the batch of Commodity being delivered from the customs territory of the Republic of Kazakhstan; 3 (ii) accept Karakuduk Commodity from the Principal at Metering Point 719 (PSP Samara of the Western branch office of KazTransOil) and arrange its transportation for export in accordance with the Delivery Basis; (iii) execute all customs formalities to carry out transit transportation of the batch of Commodity through the territory of Russian Federation and the countries of the C.I.S.;
perturbation.explanation: In Section 4.1 (i), the word 'Commodity' is changed to 'Oil', the definition of Oil and Commodity is not provided, this creates an in-text contradiction because it's not clear if 'Oil' and 'Commodity' are the same and creates ambiguity
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 At all times during the term of this Contract, the Company shall: (i) Assist in obtaining required&sbsp;official export permissions (certificate of origin of the Oil at place the load output, customs declaration on Commodity output) for release of the batch of Commodity being delivered from the customs territory of the Republic of Kazakhstan; 3 (ii) accept Karakuduk Commodity from the Principal at Metering Point 719 (PSP Samara of the Western branch office of KazTransOil) and arrange its transportation for export in accordance with the Delivery Basis; (iii) execute all customs formalities to carry out transit transportation of the batch of Commodity through the territory of Russian Federation and the countries of the C.I.S.;
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Commodity' with 'Oil' in a parenthetical clarification of export permissions. While the explanation suggests this introduces ambiguity, the provided snippets do not demonstrate a strong or direct conflict. The fact that both terms are used within the same clause ('customs declaration on Commodity output') indicates a potential overlap or interchangeability that mitigates a strong contradiction. The impact on enforceability or interpretation isn't demonstrably significant based solely on these text snippets.
